scene: You spot your crush sitting alone at the college cafeteria. Your friends are nowhere around. You have your lunch tray in hand.
opts: Walk over and casually ask if you can sit — no big deal
Do a full lap around the cafeteria pretending to look for friends, hoping they notice you
Pull out your phone and text a friend about it instead of doing anything
Go sit at another table and stare at them from a distance like a documentary filmmaker

scene: You are stuck in the lift with your crush.
opts: Start watching reels on your phone even if there is not network
Start interacting with him
Call your friends for help
Panick and press the emergency button repeatedly

scene: Your date texts you 30 minutes before meeting saying they might be 20 mins late because of traffic.
opts: Reply 'No worries, take your time!' and mean it
Reply 'No worries!' but secretly sulk the whole evening
Cancel on them immediately — punctuality is everything
Use the 20 mins to rehearse your opening conversation topics

scene: It's Friday night. Your friends want to go to a crowded house party. Your current mood is comfy pajamas and a movie.
opts: You go to the party and end up having the best night
You go but spend half the time in a quiet corner on your phone
You fake a headache and stay home guilt-free
You convince one friend to ditch the party and watch movies with you

scene: You've been texting someone you like for 2 weeks. The convo is great but they haven't asked to meet yet.
opts: You casually suggest hanging out — simple and direct
You drop hints hoping they'll get the message
You wait indefinitely and over-analyze every message
You convince yourself they're not interested and start pulling back

scene: It's 11pm on a Sunday. You have a 9am class tomorrow and your group chat just invited you for late-night street food.
opts: Go — you can nap between classes
Go, feel guilty, eat fast, rush back
Say no and actually sleep at a reasonable hour
Say yes, then cancel 15 mins before because you chickened out

scene: A new person joins your friend circle. They're funny, confident and immediately became everyone's favourite.
q: How does that make you feel?
opts: Genuinely excited — new energy is fun
A little threatened but you get over it
Uncomfortable until you've figured them out
You befriend them immediately — you're already planning a one-on-one hangout

scene: Your partner tells you they forgot your one-month anniversary. They feel terrible about it.
opts: Laugh it off — it really doesn't bother you
Tell them you're fine but you low-key needed that
Calmly say it matters to you and you'd like them to remember next time
Give them the silent treatment for two days

scene: Your semester is packed — exams, assignments and internship deadlines. Someone you like asks if you want to grab coffee this week.
opts: Yes — you'll make time, you're good at managing both
Maybe — you'll check your schedule and confirm
Not now but suggest a specific date two weeks out
No, and feel genuinely sad about it for three days

scene: You trip and fall in front of your crush in the middle of the college corridor. People saw it.
opts: Stand up, take a bow, and own the moment completely
Laugh it off quickly and walk away fast
Die a little inside but pretend you're fine
Bring it up yourself later as a funny story

scene: You're introduced to your date's close friends for the first time. The group has a lot of inside jokes you don't get.
opts: Ask about the jokes — you're genuinely curious
Smile and laugh along even when you're lost
Mostly listen and contribute when you have something good to say
Make a small joke about being the outsider to break the ice

scene: Your friend hands you the aux at a road trip. Everyone is half-asleep. You have full control.
opts: A perfectly curated playlist you made at 1am last week
Whatever's trending on Spotify right now — vibes only
A deep cut indie/underground track and hope someone asks about it
You panic and put it on shuffle — too much responsibility
